sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
All cells are gray again. The cursor is at cols 27-30, which is over the long segment. But painting produced gray, not green.
Wait, earlier when I painted with cursor at cols 29-30 (over long segment), the top-center cell became green. But now painting with cursor at cols 27-30 produced gray.
Maybe the paint color depends on the cell's current color, not the cursor position:
- If cell is gray (g): paint with gray (g) (no change)
- If cell is green (N): paint with gray (g) (toggle)
- If cell is white (W): paint with green (N)
But that doesn't match my earlier observation where painting a gray cell with cursor over long segment produced green.
Let me reconsider. Maybe the paint behavior is:
- Cursor over long segment: paint green (N)
- Cursor over short segment: paint gray (g)
- Cursor over gap: paint based on some other rule
But when I batched 3 paint actions, all cells became gray. Maybe the cursor moved during the batch?
Looking at the cursor position: cols 27-30. The long segment starts at col 27. So the cursor is at the left edge of the long segment.
Wait, let me re-examine the bar structure:
- Blue square: cols 12-16
- Short gray segment: cols 17-22
- Gap: cols 23-26
- Long gray segment: cols 27-42
The cursor is at cols 27-30, which is over the long segment. So painting should produce green.
But all cells are gray. Let me try painting one cell at a time to see what happens.
Actually, wait. Looking at the result metadata:
- animation_frame_count: 19
- animation_changed: true
- animation_changed_cell_count: 56
- animation_transition_count: 11
This suggests there were many intermediate animation frames. Maybe the batch of 3 paint actions triggered some animation that changed the board state.
Let me try painting one cell at a time and checking the result after each.
Actually, let me first try painting just one cell and see if it becomes green.

</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
